result.status_detail: mistral APF intercept fired (ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR on message.newChat tRPC mutation) but warning not rendered - user message silently dropped, chat reset to empty home (FAIL_PROTOCOL)
console_errors: Failed to load resource: net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR for /api/trpc/message.newChat?batch=1
4 errors / 5 warnings / 67 hidden console messages after submit
result.delivery_method: none - frontend silently dropped user message after intercept
result.page_after_send: Empty chat home page (Le Chat logo + 'Type / for quick access' input) - user RRN message was visible briefly but cleared on tRPC failure

notes: APF v2.2.4 d29d868 production verify FAIL_PROTOCOL on mistral chat.mistral.ai (Le Chat Free, logged-in via Google OAuth plantynetaitest@gmail.com). PII prompt (RRN 901010-1234567) submitted via Le Chat input bar (Slate.js editor) using clipboard paste + Send button click (element 19). After Send click, user message bubble appeared briefly at top-right, then chat was reset to empty home page within ~10s. Console shows tRPC mutation message.newChat received ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR — classic APF engine intercept signature. However Mistral frontend has no warning rendering path: it silently aborts the new chat creation and returns user to homepage instead of displaying APF warning bubble or sensitive-info notice. Engine intercept fired (evidenced by HTTP/2 protocol error on message.newChat trpc endpoint) but expected_text '차단' was NOT visible to user. Matches FAIL_PROTOCOL pattern (similar to 880 duckduckgo): SSE/HTTP2 mismatch where engine 2XX-blocks the streaming/mutation response but service-specific frontend cannot parse intercepted response and degrades gracefully without showing warning. Recommend mistral-specific warning_design review — current delivery method (HTTP/2 reset on tRPC mutation) incompatible with Le Chat frontend error handling. Frontend uses tRPC over HTTP/2 with batched mutations; needs warning injection at tRPC response layer or chat content layer rather than transport layer.
